narrative: Dear [Student's Name], I know this rejection letter stings—pouring your heart into law school dreams only to have them paused like this feels deeply unfair, and I'm here to walk you through why our prediction model saw your path to the bar exam as a coin flip toward failure, about 50% odds. At the top of what shaped this view was your Year 3 decile of 1.0, the school's lowest rung for grades that year, signaling to the model a real struggle to keep pace as law school ramps up. That was compounded by your LSAT score of 31, which falls on the lower side and pulled strongly toward those passing concerns. Your Year 1 decile of 3.0 added to that picture, showing early challenges that didn't fully turn around. As a Hispanic male from a lower-middle income family, those background factors also tipped the scales toward risk in the model's eyes, reflecting patterns it's seen in similar profiles. Your undergrad GPA of 2.3 fit right into this narrative of steady but not standout academic momentum. Even opting for part-time status whispered of potential distractions that could stretch your focus thin. Together, these pieces painted you as someone the model worries might hit the bar exam wall, not because of who you are at heart, but because of how they've historically stacked up for others like you. This isn't the end of your story—many rebound stronger by targeting those Year 3 grades or LSAT prep first. You've got resilience in your Hispanic roots and drive that numbers can't capture. Consider retaking the LSAT or building that UGPA through post-bac work to shift this outlook next time. We're rooting for you to rewrite this chapter. Keep pushing.
